reviews.title: Never Again!
reviews.text:
This was our first—and without question, our last—stay at this hotel. We arrived expecting the quality and professionalism associated with the Renaissance name. Instead, we walked straight into a nightmare.

Check-in was a joke. It took over 20 minutes in a lobby that felt more like a noisy night market than a Marriott property. It was pure chaos—no system, no control, no welcome. Just confusion and staff who looked overwhelmed and unprepared. When it was finally our turn, we were greeted by a front desk staff with bad breath. Yucks!

But that was only the beginning.

The key cards didn’t work. Not once or twice—but six times. We were stuck, unable to get in the lift or access our room. Each time, we had to call for help like helpless fools. The most absurd part? Being told our room was “locked from the inside” when every single one of us was still in the lobby. How does that even happen? It took over an hour, several clueless staff members, and finally a night duty manager to fix it—by then it was almost midnight. Our son was exhausted. We were exhausted. And for what? A room we paid for and couldn’t even access.

You’d think it couldn’t get worse. But it did.

We thought the pool might help us unwind—but it was yet another disaster. It’s shared with Four Points, and the scene was ridiculous. No seats, no space. Families were sitting on the floor just trying to find a spot. It was overcrowded, noisy, and completely unmanaged. Nothing about it felt like the relaxing hotel experience we were promised.

We came here looking for rest, comfort, and quality time as a family. Instead, we got stress, exhaustion, and a total lack of care from a brand that should know better. This stay wasn’t just disappointing—it was infuriating. We will never return, and I wouldn’t recommend this place to anyones. Renaissance, you should be ashamed.

reviews.title: I had an annoying experience  during check in
reviews.text: It takes a while to check in, and the late comers just went directly to the front of the line without the hotel staff calling their attention to fall in line. When it was my turn to check- in, I was informed by the front desk receptionist that I booked a "room only". I was shocked because I specifically booked a room with breakfast and I have my agoda voucher to prove it. I was allowed to check in but I was told to wait for a confirmation of my booking if it was with breakfast. Instead of an immediate feedback, I had to go down to the reception hours after, but no feedback was given. I was told to call front desk again in the morning before breakfast. The next day, I called the front desk and I was told that they will honor my agoda booking if I have a copy of my voucher, which I told them I have, and in fact They had it photocopied. It was only then that I was allowed to have breakfast. The whole thing was very annoying and it caused me embarrassment to have to beg for something that I already paid. The breakfast was average and not what was in the website pictures. All things considered, the hotel room I booked is overpriced, as the furnishings and fixtures showed signs of wear and tear. The hotel's star rating should have been 3 stars only, and I am being charitable here. The only good thing here is the side view of the Petronas Towers.

reviews.title: Worse 5 star hotel ever stayed in KL
reviews.text:
This is the worst hotel I've ever stayed in KL. I believed the east Wing is probably like a budget version of the Renaissance hotel, even though no where on their website claims otherwise.

I did not expect such poor service from a Mariott hotel chian! Will never ever go back to this hotel again!
reviews.negatives:
1. There are actually 2 wings of the hotel. Went to the west lobby, and was told they have my reservation, but I need to walk over to the east wing to check in. No help was offered etc to help us with the lugguage to the other wing. No where were we informed which wing our stay was supposed to be. 
2. On the mariott website, claimed to have high speed internet for free at the rooms, but turns out it cost RM63 for 24hrs internet access. 
3. Guest have problems with their access cards in the lift. Met at least 3 guests in the lifts that also faced problems when trying to use their room access cards in the lift.
4. We made reservation for non smoking room, but the hotel did not received any details of our preference.
5. First night the room was full, but us in a room at the same level as the swimming pool and the room condition is pathetic. The power button on the TV was missing, the wardrobe door cannot close properly and no hair dryer available.
5. Check in was terribly slow. 3 person to serve us in total for the whole check in process. First one told us to wait at the counter, and then walk away without letting us know how long to wait, etc. Then another staff was called in, check in till halfway, for no reason, got another staff to continue with the checkin while she just left and attend to something else.

reviews.title: Worst 5 star hotel
reviews.text:
This hotel is probably the worst hotel i ever stayed at Kuala Lumpur! I book the hotel from Agoda 1 week before i check in. I requested for a non-smoking room and a king size bed. However, when i check in to the hotel, the front desk staff told me the hotel are running high occupancy and couldn't arrange a room with king size bed and non-smoking room for me. Oh man! i make the reservation 1 week ago, there are running full 1 week ago? Anyway, i couldn't blame much on that, as they never promise me anything. Somehow, at last they managed to give me a room with king bed for a Rm37 upgrade to others room type.
 
The nightmare came upon us, once we step outside the corridor. The corridor carpet are very very old and the non-smoking floor smell like weed! And then, the room is far from what i expected from the price we paid! The room look very old as well as the facilities. The carpet full of stain, yes the view are marvelous which is facing the KLCC but our windows are full of fingers prints, i can't barely see the view outside the window! The aircon is hot, even i lower the temperature and higher the speed. Useless! The sheets are clean but with a old 90's blanket.
 
The facilities here is very old too, small old tv, the major problem is the internet service. From what i read from the internet guide in the tv. There are still using telephone base to connect internet, and the charges is based on telephone charges. I couldn't imagine how much could it be, if once i connect to the internet.
 
One more thing i have to mention is the bath room shower. The shower is noisy! When someone is shower, the whole room sound terrible! This is very very annoying!

reviews.title: Never visit during peak season
reviews.text:
Certainly! Here's a concise summary for your review:

---

I recently stayed at the Renaissance Kuala Lumpur and was quite disappointed with my experience. Check-in was delayed, with my rooms not ready until 6:00 PM despite a 3 PM check-in time. Additionally, my request for a room with a large bed was not fulfilled. The Kids Club, a key reason for choosing this hotel, was under reservation, and the temporary setup was unsatisfactory. These issues significantly impacted my stay and fell short of my expectations.

reviews.title: Over-rated & over-priced hotel w/discrimation
reviews.text:
It's just a 3.5 stars hotel. Staffs are on all fours for guests with white skin. Waited almost 10mins to get attended at check-in and only served a welcome drink when leaving the counter for the room in another wing, 

Had a better breakfast choices at other 3 stars hotels and their selections remains the same throughout the whole stay. Staffs at the cafe will not notice you asking for refills if your are Asian and raising your voice in the noisy cafe to get their attention will be snapped back by their staff.

The room comes with flicking toilet's light that wasn't replaced, baby roaches in the closet and smells of previous guests on the pillows. No housekeeping after the 1st night and they don't change the towels even after the 2nd night, despite taking their instruction to leave them in the bath tub. Hence, the same lot of towels were used throughout until checking out.

If you drive, they charge guests for parking at min. RM10/day. Not a huge sum, as a traveling sales person for over 2 decades and having stayed in numerous hotels all over Malaysia, this a first for me.

Would not choose stayed here if our European principal had not insisted on putting up here with their group's membership.

reviews.title: Great Location ,average service, Old hotel
reviews.text:
Upon arrival no bellman to carry our luggage , no doorman to open the door....
This is how this hotel rates it self as a 5 star..

Took me 15 minutes of waiting time in front of the front desk till the receptionist got off the phone and spoke to me

Room was very very small, very old broken furniture.

Bathroom had yellowish water coming out, 1st we were told that this cuz of the tank, than next day we were told it's a hotel wide problem.

The idea of solving the problem or having us transferred to another hotel was not even entertained.

Food is very very average, and over priced.

Concierge are very very well informed....

A tourist information center is right opposite the hotel where u can use 30 minutes of free internet, and gets lots of map, they 're open from 10 am to 10 pm.

On the same side of the road, there is NZ restaurant, just 10 minutes walk.
Furhter down next to the Suria/KLCC twin tower mall, there is a great local restaurant with superb food called NASI KANDAR.
In KLCC suria mall, there are tonnes of restaurants and fast food places as well as fine dining.

Hotel has a great location, it's faraway from the congested and overcrowded bukit bintang, but just 20-30 minutes walk to it. 

You can also take the monorail next to the hotel , 2 stops to bukit bintang for 1.4 rm per adult.

A few minutes on Jalan Ampang soon after u cross the Jalan sultan Ismail , there is the Dan Wangi LRT station, the putra line, which u can take to anywhere in KL.........to avoid the greedy taxi drivers...

Hotel has a nice/big swimming pool, but we avoided using it cuz of the water condition.
reviews.negatives:
Hotel needs renovation badly.

Lot of guests, always busy, could not find a doorman or bellman to carry my luggage, i had to carry them myself................
Furniture very very old.
Yellowish water coming out all the time with different excuses everyday given to us.

reviews.title: Good atmosphere but poor management in reception
reviews.text:
I got a good deal in the net and took the offer but it turned out to be a bad experience.
After a long flight I wanted to just crash but they made me wait at the reception for 1 hour at the check in to give me a room.
I specified I wanted a double bed but they gave me double cot and said they don't have kingsize available.i wanted a non smoking but it turned out to be a smoking floor and the whole floor was stinking. The rooms were shabby and old and dirty. I tried to complain but they never pick up the phone at the reception , I tried for 2 hours and the operator failed to get back after several attempts to remind them.i had to go back to reception and complain after waiting for half hour there to meet a staff who are always busy. Lack of staff at the reception area.i
My experience there was a overall disaster. Be carefull do not have high expectation and don't expect even a 5 star experience. It's a 4 star only.

reviews.title: Needs upkeep of basic amenities
reviews.text: We booked this hotel because the wedding we were attending was being held here. We thought this would be a convenient option as we have a 17 month old. It looked like a nice enough hotel from the internet photos, being part of the Marriott group. Upon check-in, the staff were friendly. We arrived in our room to find no running water when we tried to flush the toilet. I tried the taps in the shower, sink and bathtub, there was no water. The tap in the bath nearly came off the wall when I pulled it (maintenance required!!). I rung reception and found out that they were well aware that there was a problem with water supply and it was being looked at. Yet, they failed to let us know when we had just checked in a few minutes earlier. The water came back within the hour but was very brown. We let it run for a while, it was still quite brown. We rang reception again and they said to let it run for a while longer (another 30mins, still brown). There was no way our child was getting bathed in this. Many of the lights in the room were also not in working order (?busted light globes). Very disappointed, we went down to reception and demanded for a room change - to one that had clean water and lighting. We were kindly upgraded to a one bedroom suite. My brother arrived the next day - he too had brown water, a broken TV and some non-working lights. My parents arrived two days later for the wedding and checked into a room with brown water also. I think this hotel is in need of some serious maintenance on their water pipes or they should invest in some kind of water filter. We stayed for three nights. On the last day, we got the daily service of our room as usual, and the sheets were changed but were replaced with smelly, "unfresh" bed linen ie. the water smell you get when the laundry hasn't been dried properly in winter. By then, we were over it and just put up with it.

reviews.title: Bad Experience at Renaissance KL
reviews.text:
I booked 4 rooms for a 2-night stay for myself and friends, each room with different names for ease of registration.  During check-in, the staff messed up the names with keys issued to the wrong person.  One of my friends had even gone thru the registration process not once not twice but three times and only got his room at 4pm even though we reached the hotel before 12noon.  Front-desk staff not experienced nor well-trained for their job.  When asked if there is any soccer-channel, the silly remark was 'I have not stayed in the hotel room before, I don't know'.

Beware of power-trips, all of us consistently experienced multiple power-trips throughout the stay.
reviews.negatives: Poor front-desk service.

reviews.title: Very Disappointing Renaissance Stay
reviews.text:
Front desk staff are very slow and rude. They always finished up their computer work first rather than attending to guests who are already waiting in front of them. The worst of customer service by the staff. These happened to me during check-in, at the Concierge - when I want to ask for directions and even during the check-out. Very rude of them!
Furthermore, I have to pay RM10 every day of my stay for carpark. No Complimentary parking!
I have requested for non-smoking room way in advance and yet I was given one with strong smell of cigerette smoke! Asked for a change but was not entertained.To make it worse, most of the lift lobby and corridors smell of cigerette smoke too!
This will be my first & last stay in Renaissance!! I would never recommend anybody to stay there!

reviews.title: Great for Kids, But Not Ideal for Restful Staycations
reviews.text:
The main attraction of this hotel is the large pool, which is perfect for children, especially during school holidays. In that aspect, it delivers. The pool area is lively and well-suited for families with young kids.

However, if you're hoping for a quiet and restful staycation, this may not be the best choice. The overall environment is noisy, and the windows are not soundproof. Whistles are blown regularly by pool staff for safety, from the moment the pool opens until it closes. While safety is important, the constant whistle-blowing can be frustrating for those trying to rest.

Even in a pool-facing room on a lower floor, I could still hear motorcycles from the nearby road. Perhaps I was just unlucky with the room location, but the noise made it difficult to relax.

The staff are polite but seem inexperienced or under-trained. I received repeated apologies without clear explanations or proper solutions. I tried calling the front desk after waiting almost 20 minutes for a lift, but no one answered, which added to the frustration.

I also had difficulty getting a higher-floor room at check-in, even though I had requested it in advance. After multiple rounds of communication and pressure, the hotel eventually agreed to move me to a higher floor. It seems they may only have a duty manager available at night, or the day staff are just too overwhelmed to handle such requests. The lack of manpower made it difficult to address the issue promptly.

The poolside was very wet and slippery. I slipped once and would advise anyone with young children to be extra careful.

Overall, while this hotel might be fun for a short family getaway with kids, I would not recommend it to guests looking for peace, comfort, or consistent service. Based on my experience, I genuinely don’t understand how it has received such high ratings on various platforms.

reviews.title: Very old hotel with horrible prices
reviews.text:
I was extremely dissapointed with my visit at the Renassaince hotel. The pictures from the website seemed to have fooled me as they clearly do not represent the hotel in its current state - its in desperate need of renovation. I would rate this a four star hotel at most. Food is very poor and ridiculously expensive - for example, a sandwhich and a soda at the pool cafe cost me RM 80+. I am not a cook but seriously, I can easily do better and I tried several of their dishes. Their Cesar Salad is the most lame attempt I've ever seen. And please do not use their cleaning service. Two shirts and a pair of jeans cost me RM 86, this was not dry cleaning but regular laundry...
On the positive side, the staff was great and very helpful throughout my visit. Yet again, I cannot justify paying the same amount as any five star hotel just for this. There are so many other better options, I suggest you avoid this until they do a makeover.

reviews.title: Rooms dirty & mouldy,  front reception not helpful
reviews.text:
We were a party of 6 travelling for 2 weeks through Malaysia and Thailand. A family of 2 Adults, 2 Children a Grandmother and a Friend. We booked accomodation in Kuala Lumpur with Agoda and both times had issues with getting what we booked. At the Renaissance, we booked months in advance and purchased 3 superior king / twin rooms with buffet breaky.

I emailed them and explained our travelling situation and requested 3 rooms close together with King beds. When we arrived we got 3 twin rooms, on the same floor but miles away from each another, after having a few words with the reception they finally agreed to change one of our rooms to a King and the room was located directly across from one of the initial rooms we also occupied, so they could have given this to us to start with. 

Our Agoda reservation paperwork clearly stated Breakfast Included, but they initailly refused to honour this. After debating the situation for a good 15 minutes, and having them tell me to take it up with our travel company, Agoda, I finally requested to speak with the food and beverage manager
and they finally said it was ok for us to have breakfast. 

We were offered an upgrade but unfortunately this was offered on the morning of the day we were departing! Our flight left late in the evening so we all moved into 1 room and asked for a late checkout on this 1 room. They said it was ok but charged us a 1/2 night at the standard room rate for the hotel not even on what we paid through agoda! Considering all the issues we had, and that they offered us a room upgrade that we couldn't use,  you would think that they might have let us stay in that 1 room for no charge. The room itself, the curtains were dirty and had cigarette holes in them, contrary to us having a NON smoking room and signs in the room saying there was a fine for being caught smoking in the rooms. The bathroom was full of mould! Bed and linen was confortable and clean though which was nice.
reviews.positives: Pool and pool deck area was big and clean. The Buffett breaky had a huge amount to choose from.

Cleaning was ok. Breakfast too poor,one day no orange juice, other day, black coffe was so bad that we only had water or pure milk.

reviews.title: Not worth it
reviews.text:
Overall our stay at the Rennaisance Kuala Lumpur was a bad start into a Malaysia Holiday. We - a family of three with our teenage daughter - were traveling with a wheelchair, due to a physical handicap of one in our party. We arrived at the West Wing of the hotel and were informed upon presentation of the pre-payment confirmation of AGODA that we were actually booked to a room in the East Wing. That would actually be quite a way around the building and the room there would be very small - especially regarding the wheelchair requirement. So we were offered to upgrade against additional payment to a Executive-Club Status with a handicapped friendly large bath room. The new rate would include sumptious breakfast in the club lounge, internet access and a range of additiona benefits. We were tired and did not want to again move around the place, hence we agreed to the offer.

We ended up in a smokers hole far from the executive floor, with a spaciuos bathroom - true -but featuring no place to put amenities - not even the toothbrush. Things like glass for toothbrushing was to placed on the closed toilet seat lid. The room had one bathtub, but it would be impossible for a paraplegic to get into it. No handles and no other aids available.
While we were three people and also had to pay extra for the 3rd (quite uncomfortable folding) bed, we did not get third set of towels, water, coffee etc. Only after 3 requests we received at least additional towels.

The hotel is a nightmare for people in wheelchairs and even the main restaurant and breakfast area are not accessible by wheelchair. The promised (and paid) internet was down most of the time and asking someone only resulted in: I will ask engineering. They never replied.

The whole stay was a nightmare an we cannot honestly recommend the Rennaisance as a high class hotel to stay in Kuala Lumpur. Compared to a 2 Star Category establishment (measured against the quality we experienced) it is in our opinion  definitly ex
reviews.positives: The location is quite central and all sight and shopping areas are within easy reach. A Monorail stop is just accross the road
reviews.negatives:
The hotel is pretty much "lived out", i.e. it is not only old, but looks even older. The level and competence of service leaves a lot to desire in the sense, that people are always smiling, trying to sell additional cost items of services, but are finding it very difficult to react to the things you are asking for.
Breakfast in the club lounge was poor in terms of choices, and partly in quality.

reviews.title: overrated for this price
reviews.text: Overall the hotel is a bit overrated. interior of room is a bit aged and old. I recently stayed at Melia, which is a 3 star hotel, and i can say it pretty much out perform Rennaisannce in terms of value for money and location. Little things like shampoo and bath gel are not provided for. there are no hairdryer. no buffet breakfast. Only good point about location is that it's close to the monorail and within walking distance to the Petronas towers.

reviews.title: Old Renaissance Hotel
reviews.text: With the location in Kuala Lumpur and the attractive price for a 5-star hotel it is a luring offer for any foreigner to stay. However Renaissance hotel seems to be an old hotel without much attention in maintenance for the rooms. First of all the lighting is poor which results in dark room and some lights are not even working such as the wardrobe light. Location is actually around the center of KL but is quite a distance to walk to the shopping area. Overall not very satisfied with the hotel and will not stay again unless maybe it is being renovated.
reviews.positives: an attractive price for a 5-star hotel
reviews.negatives: Hotel rooms are old and abit rundown, with some lighting not working and the room phone making funny noises

reviews.title: Not worthy of the five star rating
reviews.text: Don't get fooled by what looks like a great deal for a five star hotel. This hotel is in serious need of a facelift and renovation. The furniture in the rooms and lobby appear to be 20 years old and our bathroom was not in very good condition either. We had to ask for our sheets and towels to be changed because they too were old and stained (even blood stains on some towels). The room was musty and the air seemed stale. It was also too warm as the aircon wasn't strong enough even when set on high at the lowest temperature. The hotel is very close to the monorail (Bukit Nanas stop) but isn't as centrally located as the hotels two stops down the line at Bukit Bintan. We thought we were getting a great deal on a five star hotel but actually you are better off at staying at a three star boutique hotel in Bukit Bintan even though the latter is slightly more expensive. When we complained about the condition of the hotel we were told the hotel was two decades old and there hadn't been any renovations. We were told renovations will start soon, working from the top down. Until they are completed I wouldn't give any consideration to staying at the Renaissance Kuala Lumpur.
reviews.positives: close to monorail station, good dim sum restaurant, large swimming pool
reviews.negatives: old furniture, musty rooms, not close to any shopping, poor air quality, extra charges for several amenities

Then he walk to counter, but he also ignore my message, he didnt pass the message to the team that they should served who Quee first, and next guest service (chinese female)served to another guy, so I walk to the counter and drop my key, I said to her that I have been waiting here for 20 min, even before all the these guy arrived.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: For a 5 star hotel, they have a third party handling their parking. It's just absurd! U have to pay parking everyday! Worse still, though I booked room with breakfast, I was told I have to pay for it till I had to show them proof of booking. What if I didn't have it with me that time?! I also made reservation of high tea which was confirmed via email and when I went there, it was not even in their schedule. So overall, not really up to the standards I would say!

reviews.title: General feedback
reviews.text:
Room condition - quite old and run down, faded carpet and dust smell all over the room
Check-in/checked-out - not that efficient, causing customer to queue and waited for within 10 minutes before being attended. Should consider having more staff/facilities at check-in counter or pre-on line check in.
Parking - need to validate the parking card every time going in and out, considering the East Wing and West Wing are quite a distance, making customer to every time have to walk to check in counter is quite displeasure.
The above are not within the expectation of a 5 star hotel

reviews.title: Room conditions
reviews.text: Hotel too old. AC not cold. Floor trap too slow to discharge.  Condition run down. Pillow not comfortable.

reviews.title: Torn-out wallpapers, dirty carpets, poor internet
reviews.text: The air-con was broke and we had to switch our room.  Fire door was kept wide open.  Poor internet connectivity.  Torn-out wallpapers, dirty carpets and only 1 pair of slippers provided.  Finally, some light bulbs did not lit up in both of the rooms we stayed in.

reviews.title: A worn down hotel that is really a four star hotel
Unclean, Unfriendly
reviews.text:
Unfriendly and non-welcoming staff/reception.
Location is just OK.
Rooms are not so clean. It is quite old building.
Breakfast is good quality, but again unfriendly staff!
reviews.positives: Breakfast is good. Location is OK.

reviews.title: Very poor performance of the front desk people
reviews.text: I requested for double bed, non-smoking room. On arrival at 2 AM they requested rudely to wait for unlimited period to have this type of room. After I talk with Bangkok people over phone for at least 30 min, one higher official attended me and upgraded my room. This was a disgusting matter while I was traveling with family, including children. Moreover the front desk people was very rude, unprofessional and inefficient. This was the most nuisance situation for me ever since I become a member. Their smart card key does not work in 80% of the times. I had to change my card three times during my 3 days stay. Even though it was not working. I will not recommend anybody to stay in this hotel unless they upgrade their front desk people.
reviews.positives: Breakfast, Internet and Room light switches

reviews.title: Good location and very clean lobby
reviews.text:
This is a very big and popular hotel. so it makes it easy to tell cab drivers where to go. Upon arrival at 2am. Our rooms were not ready in which we had to wait 1 and a half hours to sort out. Although we had a big group which required 6 rooms, we had booked these rooms over a month in advance, which i see no reason for them to not have our rooms ready. Being late and tired from the plane trip we expected high rating hotels like these to have things organised. by 3:30 am we finally got to our rooms to find that it was a little bit under our expectations. We had complaints of rooms being dirty and faulty hot water and lighting systems. Telephones didnt work. Bed comfort was poor and the magnetic room keys werent working well as some of us got stuck in the lift trying to operate it when our swipe card was faulty. 
The only good thing about this place is the location and the pool. Room service i feel is a little bit too expensive for the quality of food being served and also the room standards were poor and out of date. 
The location is good for those who dont mind the hot weather as the twin towers or pavillion shopping mall is a 20 min walk away. For those peak traffic times walking maybe the easier option
reviews.positives: Excellent Wifi. walking distance to shops

reviews.title: Definitly not one of the best 4-5 stars hotel
reviews.text:
Definitly not one of the best 4-5 stars hotel I've stayed in.

During our check-in the concierge was a little bit too pushy for selling an upgrade.

When we entered the room it smelled a little bit, at first we thought it just was a little shut in and turn on the AC, when we arrived back later in the evening it had gotten worse, called the reception, came a cleaner who cleaned a little bit around the refrigerator. When we woke up it still smelled but not as bad, again it got much worse later in evening, this time  they came up and change the whole refregirator, seemed to have leeked some in the back. 3rd day it stilled smelled, by then we we're about to change rooms, but we had only one night left and was not gonna stay in the room for that long and had a busy schedule.

Positive parts was they have a great gym, one of the best I've seen in a hotell since they also sold membership to private people. 

The Breakfast had a lot to chose from, the food was quite good, not splendid, but I would say above average.

Pool-area was nice, with alot of beds, just the pool was to cold.
reviews.positives: Nice breakfast, huge gym, nice pool area

reviews.title: Very disappointed
reviews.text:
Alas , very dissappointed! After checking after 5 pm my room was discusting , had not been cleaned bed not made rubbish in bins on floor and all over the bathroom. Was moved to another room where over three days it was cleaned only once and i was supplied with one bath towel and told sorry they had run out of clean ones!! 
overall was not very happy at all at the standards
reviews.positives: in walkable distance of main shopping malls and the twin towers and embassies. Hard rock cafe just around the corner , as is the sky train

reviews.title: Disappointed, below expected standard.
reviews.text:
This hotel turns out to be fulled of group tourists.  Front office staff cannot handle when they check in altogether. Besides they are not polite and unable to give clear information / explanation to questions when asked.  Inconsistency in replies from one staff to another, which makes it confusing for guests.  Personally we will not stay here again.
With many groups of tourists, breakfast became a market where guests fight for tables and chairs....
reviews.positives: This is not a four star hotel, for the rate we paid and service received.

reviews.title: 5 Star Hotel with 1 Star Service
reviews.text:
Upon check-in, we asked reception about the HSBC / Agoda promotion, which was supposed to have a complimentary room upgrade in April.  The reception, Daisy, had no idea, claiming it was Agoda's promotion and nothing to do with the hotel.  I requested the hotel to double check and they agreed.  In the meantime, they gave me a room and said they would get back to us ASAP.   We went to the room for one hour (as there was a thunderstorm outside) and then left to explore KL until evening.  We happened to walk by "Daisy" on our return but she didn't say anything.  That was the last we heard from  her.  So much for follow up service by the hotel.

The next morning, at check-out, we stood at the reception whilst the hotel staff ignored us whilst busily using the computer.  It was not until we loudly said check-out she coldly took our key card with an OK.  Very impolite.

The rooms are large but the condition was quite worn - the room could do with some renovation.  We stayed in the East Wing.  The safe didn't work also.

reviews.title: Below average
reviews.text:
We were given the room in the east wing of the hotel. I think it's the old section of the hotel.

The major depressing thing was the internet charges (RM 24 for an hr). Hotels all over the world consider internet as a utility like water and electricity and it's free. I am not able to understand what value addition Renaissance is providing over and above to justify such atrocious charges.

I had to call up the house-keeping every day to bring in new towels. They took away wet towels and never replaced it. They never replaced complementary bottled water – I had to call them again. There was no news paper. I don’t whether I had to pay extra for the paper.
reviews.negatives: Old facilities and below average services

reviews.title: Go elsewhere
reviews.text:
We booked this hotel for use before going on to Thailand. They are doing some rooms up and we expected some noise but nothing like the cacophany of hammering we experienced.We were moved to a slightly better room but not an upgrade.
No contact from management regarding noise or inconvenience. very poor attitude i felt.
reviews.negatives: Noise,hotel rooms were poor.lack of attention to complaints

reviews.title: Bad Experience
reviews.text:
Book 4 rooms for myself and friends, Front desk staff messed up two with same surname.  One of my friends went through 3 rounds of registration process due to staff's carelessnes and got his room at 4pm even though we arrived at aroung noon.

Poor knowledge of hotel's services and bad people sskill, when ask if there is any soccer-channel the silly remark from the staff was "how would I know, I have never stay in the hotel".

Beware of power-trips, encountered several power-trips throughout stay.  When engineering saff came to fix power, he came without ladder or high-stool even though he knew the circuit-breaker box was night the ceiling.  Imagine he standing on the room-safe box, with one hand hanging on the ledge and the other trying to replace the fuse.

Will not return to this hotel, ever !
reviews.negatives: Poor Front desk operation

reviews.title: Renaissance Kuala Lumpur Hotel
reviews.text: the staff very sweet
reviews.negatives: every thing be ok

reviews.title: Old hotel
reviews.text: I stay in East Wing, it is a old hotel with old decoration and even the wall paper is falling down. I just can say everything is old and don't expect too much. The coffee shop close before dinner time!!!
reviews.negatives: Old building, old hotel

reviews.title: Not 5 star service
reviews.text: Check in was slow. Only 1 or 2 person working. Took me 25 minutes. Room run down and bed is not comfortable. Blanket makes my body itchy.
reviews.room_type: Deluxe, Guest room, 1 King, Sofa bed

reviews.title: Bad customer service and room problems
reviews.text: Started with check in problem . Because of early flight got there around 12:30pm . Requested early check in but didn’t have room available and asked me to wait. Also even paid for a family of 4 he asked me to pay ~100 per night for additional guests but we were only 4. Upon telling him for family suite for 4 room was booked. He didn’t handle it right. Waited till 3pm their scheduled check in time . Still no room ready. Upon checking with reception asked to wait till 3:40. Finally got room . When went to room it smelled like someone had been smoking in the room when it supposed to be non smoking room as we had requested. On 3rd day ceiling in bathroom started leaking. House keeping knew and they had left a towel under the area where it was leaking but leak wasn’t taken care of until we came back to room at night and I called. Also they didn’t have any help to call taxi  for us and they had their own guy standing outside who charged me MYR 225 to airport which I would have paid  MYR105

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
The so-called "Navigators" who man reception couldn't navigate their way out out of a 7-Eleven. Front of house was chaotic, during a three day stay we must have spent a combined 5 hours standing at the reception desk whilst various staff and trainees tried to work out what they were doing. 

Breakfast was fabulous and the rooms were nice. Be cautious of being put in the West Wing, due to refurbishments (which there is no warning of) you have to follow signs down various corridors to get to the West Wing from the East Wing, which is tedious and annoying, taking 10 minutes at least just to get to your room. 

Location was also great, just such a shame about the check-in experience (which took 45 minutes) and the constant shambles that is front of house organisation and management. It wouldn't have been so bad if we were made to feel welcome, kept informed or dealt with promptly/ in a friendly manner. 

Totally not worth the money paid and there are far better options nearby.

reviews.title: Great disappointment
reviews.text:
The location of the hotel is not too bad, having to know the situation of traffic jam.  
What disappointed me is the hotel.  The looby looks great, but the room is bad.  Water sewage chock, ants found on bed, room lighting is dim.

reviews.title: need to change alot
reviews.text: my stay was for 1 night. bathroom was worst part ever bcoz the water stucked due to some problems and it was damn stinky smell. the room card couldn't read proper few minutes once  need to adjust it. when I went to receipent to ask with the staff regarding it there was no proper reply from them. all lights are based which u need to touch in the system for them to on n off. but only few buttons were working.  the rest not working so it was difficult for us. there is no mini bar in the room as was mentioned early in the facility of in room. so disappointed paying rm300 for per night but not clean n systematic

reviews.title: Disappointing for a 5-star hotel
reviews.text: We went to Malaysia for a short visit and as per information online, this hotel is one of the best around the area close to the Petronas tower. True enough that it is close to Petronas. a five-minute walk from the hotel. Room is nice as it should be; clean and neat. Staff are accommodating  but not that friendly. Nothing special that had happened during our stay. No extra mile or effort from the staff. Food is ordinary.

reviews.title: Just OK
reviews.text:
My mum and I stayed in East Wing and I would say that the hotel is an obvious unrefurbished and old.  The towels are not in good condition,  telephones are 1950's model where if being used you will hear and irrrky sound (grounded).   Bathrobes are like 20years old,  bathtub-you won't dare to soak as it looks haunted,  filthy. 

Our overall stay was just OK.  Staff are nice except the lady hostess in the restaurant for bfast.  We asked her if she could take me and my mum a photo instead of saying certainly she showed us a negative body gestures that as if she didn't want to do it.  Like duhhh.  Good thing that her colleagues were there and had to push her to do so coz they saw me about to complain.  

I haven't seen any managers around to welcome the guests.  They're invisible or maybe will just go out when somebody complain. 

Not a bad hotel though it's just ok but shouldn't be rated as 5star for me.

reviews.title: No pork bacon, sausages available
reviews.text: No English breakfast, if you are Muslim great place, otherwise...

reviews.title: Not at all worthy of 5 stars for what you get!
reviews.text:
After booking on Agoda in the morning and arriving at the hotel after midday, they couldn't find my booking and had to secure a room on my credit card. Therefore check-in and check-out cost around 45 minutes each. 
The breakfast is very good with a lot of variety. The pool is also great with lovely scenery. 
The hotel as a whole is not at all worthy of five stars. We have stayed at other 3* hotels in KL and their room was better! We also stayed at the Sheraton down the road, in a club room with a huge amount of extras and facilities, and that was even cheaper then here. We won't be staying here again based on the price and the room that you get for your money.

reviews.title: not satisfy
reviews.text: not clean very bad smell in the room

reviews.title: Desperate need of renovation
reviews.text:
I stayed here 9 years ago until  now 2016 and the rooms were the same. They need to update for the price they charge and to call themselves a 5 star hotel .
Much cheaper option in the vicinity.

reviews.title: Renaissance KL Family Trip
reviews.text:
The overall room condition not reflecting as 5 stars. A bit dusty here and there.
Bath room condition also not reflecting as 5 stars.

reviews.title: Overrated hotel with terrible location.
reviews.text: Room was comfortable but won't return.
reviews.positives: Only the room, bathtub was clogged and took some time to drain.

reviews.title: Disappointed Stay
reviews.text:
Checked in almost 1am on 9th Sept onky to find toilet flush broke down at about 1.30am! Couldn't allow attendants to attend unless "it" was flushed, I spent all almost 20 mins figuring how to flush. It was almost 2am by the time I managed!!
Hot water from tap and shower was uncontrollable!

reviews.title: Checked in and breakfast was included ,
reviews.text: Receptionist Ansari must be taught how to talk  and  to treat hotel guest , he was so arrogant unprofessional in his attitude ....,I wanted to get in to the business centre to make an adjustment on my ticket , refused point blank , I walked over to the west wing and Nina was so helpful , a perfect example on how to treat hotel guest and the next morning Kumar at the concierge ,  advice him that the hotel guest who are non Muslim except to be treated as human beings and the same way he treat Muslim guest , regardless we also pay what the hotel want us to pay , how could a hotel have this joker in the front desk , do not think he is fit to be in the garbage room either
reviews.positives: West wing night receptionist Nina if I am not mistaken brilliant employee went out of her way to open business centre and help at night , east wing night receptionist Ansari  was pathetic ,

reviews.title: Not Responsive
reviews.text:
It begins with friendly front desk staff during check in at West Wing counter, as I went to wrong tower. The lady was very patience with all my requests and queries. My purpose of the stay is to attend my friend wedding dinner at Renaissance KL. 

Room condition is ok but there are nails on the carpet next to the toilet entrance every time i walk in the toilet and they poke. This is very serious matter if guest get cuts. 

However, around 6 am there was water leakage from the ceiling and I reported to the operator about this.While waiting for the maintenance team, i have to turned off the air conditioner. As it has been taken a while for the maintenance team to arrive, i called the operator to ask for a change of room and the operator told me that it is the reception matter then transfer the call to reception. Unfortunately, i waited for a while and no one pick up from the reception which I was very upset. I expect a "5 Stars" hotel guest service team would have initiate a solution for the guest to change the room for me instead of do nothing and didn't pick up my call. I called the operator a few time to get them to transfer the call to reception, at last they answer my call and trying to source for room for the swap.
It appears to me that the water leaking is a common matter in the room.  When the maintenance team is about complete the fixing work, i received a call from reception to offer to do a room swap in different floor. 

Personally think that 
(1)nails on carpet 
(2)water leaking is not allowed for a "5 Stars" hotel.

reviews.title: Not Up To Marriott Standards
reviews.text: Only stayed at this hotel as was attending a function. Check in was slow,arrived at 1.30pm requested Non smoking  King bed, was offered smoking twin bed....eventually got our request but had to come back after 4pm. Was not informed where or what time breakfast was served, or how parking for in house guests was subsidized;OK for us as we live in Malaysia and know how these things work but for tourists very poor information.Room was very tired, could definitely do with some modernizing and decoration. Bed was comfortable, buffet breakfast was quite good, staff were friendly. Sorry to say, I wouldn't stay here again,much better options in KL for better value.

reviews.title: A bit old fashioned hotel
reviews.text:
It is just like other business hotel. nothing's special.
However, I appreciate that the management positively considerred my request of opening room window (locked for safely reason) for fresh air and finally send two maintaneance personnel and opened it for me.

reviews.title: Lovely room with great view~
reviews.text:
we had a 3d2n stay in this hotel. check in was fine even though we reached there before 2pm. the staff at the reception counter was friendly and helpful. our room was granted to our request.

the pros was that the superior room was spacious enough and cosy. it had a good view of the petronas towers whether is day or night. free wifi throughout our stay. no disturbing noise at that time. easy traveling with the nearest monorail station. the bell service was good and greeted us friendly. 

the cons was that the bathroom amenities did not include toothbrush set due to out of stock. we had managed to get one set and mouthwash from them. the standing shower sink seemed to drain very slowly and flood up to our ankle area. so, we had to wait for awhile in order not to increase the level. the tv channel had less variety of shows and noticed that will begin to have blurry screen around noon. the breakfast was normal and also less variety at east wing. nothing special. walking distance to petronas twin towers was quite far and estimated about 10-15 mins.

overall, it was a good experience and enjoyable trip.

reviews.title: Lousy Hotel
reviews.text:
This is my second stay at this hotel. First time was fine. This recent stay was terrible. Whatever can go wrong went wrong.

Drainage at bathtub is stuck despite repeated feedback. Front desk staff were rude and housekeeping team tend to be forgetful.

Apart from the fact that this hotel is near to Bukit Nanas Monorail station, there is basically nothing much attractive.

reviews.title: Ok hotel, but not worth the price
reviews.text:
Overall, an OK hotel. The staff were not particularly friendly or helpful, but assisted us when we asked. The rooms were small and basic. 

The gym in this hotel is excellent and very well equipped. The pool area is also very nice. The location is fantastic.
reviews.positives: Location to KLCC and MRT is excellent

reviews.title: Value for money
reviews.text: Good service from friendly staff> Got upgraded to a larger room. Problem with drainage in hotel room as the shower area was unable t drain properly thus leaving the bathroom flooded> It wasn't until midnight the technicians arrived and decided it was a bit too late to fix the problem.

reviews.title: great location but service is poor
reviews.text: i stayed one night, had a bad experience to check in.  it took so long to get thing sorted.  the hotel itself is too big but lack of staff working at the front desk.

reviews.negatives: TV is too old. Had a cut on my finger while Nopening the bathroom glass door. Need more maintenance.

reviews.title: Bad room, nice staff
reviews.text: The hotel made a mistake during check-in. The staff gave the key of a not yet checked out room to us, and we were shocked when we saw other's luggage and clothes after we had opened the door. Luckily there was nobody in the room at that moment. However, the room condition was still poor after they had changed a new room to us. Huge black marks were on the mirror and windows, and there were leftovers on the floor and desk. On the other hand, the staff are polite and helpful.
reviews.positives: Good location, just a 7 minutes walk to the Twins Tower. Staff are friendly and helpful
reviews.negatives: Poor room condition. Furniture are old and the room is dirty.

reviews.title: Renaissance Kuala Lumpur Hotel
reviews.text:
Once we arrived they told us 1st that the only available room with kingsize bed is in the smoking floors. Instead of getting a view of the towers we got a view of the swimming pool. Because the floors are low there was noise from the pool bar & the construction. Room was very small.
I would only recommande this place for business or budget traveller who won't stay much inside.
reviews.positives: Very close to the Petronas twin towers which has the exotic Suria KLLC mall.
reviews.negatives: Furniture is very old. No separate shower in bathroom.Noise due to some construction.

reviews.title: My experience with Malaysia's hotel, Renaissance
reviews.text: the weather was not too good and making it impossible or me to enjoy the pool, which I was so looking forward to. Lastly the 24 hour gym service does not include the jacuzi, sauna, steam room service and this limits during 10 am to 10pm, a time when I am out and about seeing KL.
reviews.positives: 5 star, location in the golden triangle, imposing views of Petronas and adjacent surrounding area, first class service by the staff, services such as a top swimming pool on the 4th floor, 24 hr gymnasium, jacuzi, sauna and steam room, top restaurants
reviews.negatives: A number of services were nota available, room service entering the room when not called, very small room in comparison to the 5 star billing, very expensive meals in restaurant

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
I am writing to express my dissatisfaction and disappointment regarding my current stay at your hotel.

I had emailed your hotel approximately two to three weeks in advance to request birthday decorations for my daughter. Additionally, on the morning of 1 January 2026, before check-in, I made a follow-up phone call to reconfirm the birthday decoration arrangement and also requested a room with a KL Tower view.

When I arrived at the hotel at around 4:00 PM to check in, your staff informed me that the room was not ready and asked me to wait for about 20 minutes, assuring me that I would be contacted once the room was ready. However, I waited for almost one hour without receiving any update from the staff. My husband then had to approach the counter for the second time to enquire about the room, especially since I was feeling unwell at that time. Only then were we given the room key.

During check-in, I reconfirmed the birthday decoration request and was told that it had been noted. I also reconfirmed the request for a KL Tower view room. Your staff member, Ms. Bibi, asked whether it would be acceptable if the room was noisy due to street motorcycle activity. Both my husband and I agreed, as we were under the impression that we would be given the requested KL Tower view room.

Throughout the walk to the room, I was genuinely excited as I was looking forward to surprising my daughter, even with a simple birthday setup. Unfortunately, upon entering the room, it was clear that the room did not meet my request. This was extremely disappointing.

For your information, I am staying at your hotel for two nights (1 January 2026 – 3 January 2026). However, due to this experience, I feel very disappointed and uncomfortable about continuing my stay for the remaining nights.

I hope the management will take this matter seriously and provide clarification and appropriate action regarding this issue.
reviews.room_type: Deluxe, Guest room, 1 King, Sofa bed

reviews.title: Noisy room
reviews.text: Room that assign to me is terrible noisy with super loud vehicles especially motorbike riders! Until 5am the sound wasn't stop and we wasn't sleep nicely that night with my baby. My room type is facing kl tower. The poolside bed is not sufficient enough, the facilities are sharing btw four points and Renaissance, can you imagine you got no place to put your belongings whreby the poolside bed is limited.

reviews.title: Unpleasant stay
reviews.text: Bath tub stopper not working, had to call a technician to fix it, and it took the technician more than half an hour to arrive, response time is super slow. Next, I found out that conditioner is empty when I was bathing. Refilling amenities is a basic thing to do even for lower class hotels. Im not sure why Renaissance can miss such basic standards. Next, coffee machine broken down before I could use it. Had to call the hotel again, they did give us a new machine but slow response time from the team again. Next, the base of the toilet bowl leaks water every time I flush. Next, the soundproofing is very bad. The mat rempit was very loud at 2am. Next, I gave all these feedback to the front desk during checkout but he looked very uninterested to listen. He just wrote down what I said on a paper and didn’t provide any verbal comforts. With that attitude Im not even sure if he keyed in my feedback into the system for future improvements or not. Overall just so disappointed with this stay and with the front desk guy too.

Dont recommend to have lower floors room.

reviews.text: many better hotel around in KL. Will not be back again.

reviews.title: Windowless Rooms one and all
reviews.text: The staff in this hotel is really nice, but we were compelled to check out early, the rooms were extremely small and there were no windows, they told us the entire hotel had not a single room with windows. The room service wasn't great either, no one picked the phone when we were trying to order food to eat in the tiny room, which we decided against and had to travel a few kms to find something to eat. There is no restaurant in the hotel, just a small space for people to eat downstairs, can fit 4 most likely. When we were pulling up to the hotel we were a little concerned as it seemed like it was a poor locale, but the hotel itself was in a slightly nicer place. The main advantage is that it is close to the airport, we chekced out and hung out at the airport.

reviews.text: Cant imagine i saw 2 cockroach beside my bed. And your cleaning staff around 10pm still clean the next room n feel noisy.  I stayed few times this hotel. This is my first time experience this.

reviews.title: A dissapointing experince
reviews.text:
I feel as if we were given the worst room because we booked though Agoda. 
The staff weren't very helpful, the shower in the room seemed to have water restrictors in it that all the water that came out was a dribble and the breakfast seemed to cater for every nationality except westerners. Overall was a disappointing 3 nights.

reviews.title: Not worth for money
reviews.text:
Except the location is good--close to public transportation and tourist attractions are within walking distance. And good room service, nice food and big swimming pool.  

However, the hotel is not within the expectation of 5 stars hotel. 

Room conditions--Very disappointing. The room is old, the carpet is dusty and faded. Bed sheet even has a dirt mark. Overall, the room doesn't meet 5 stars standard, could be better. 

For a 5 stars hotel, the breakfast selection is quite limited, could have more choices. 

Staffs are nice and helpful, but they still need training and be more professional to meet 5 stars hotel standard. 

Lastly, the hotel needs refurbishment. Will return if it's refurbish, or else won't recommend for a 5 stars hotel.

reviews.title: Not impressed
reviews.text:
The hotel looks very grand and from what I have heard it has been very grand but we were not impressed, specially food-wise. The breakfast is so-so. Quality products but the chef doesnt seem to know what he is doing with anything else but asian food. No freshly made juices but some quite horrible pre-mix.

After tasteless dim-sum at Dynasty we both had to spend the rest of the day in the room with upset stomachs. The bars followed the same standard with poorly mixed cocktails and drinks and not even a decent selection of beers/alternatives.

Beyond that nothing was impressive except perhaps the bed which I found very comfortable. The housecleaning guy impressed with managing to give us 6 shampoo an no bath gel every damn time. Even when I specifically told him I wanted two bath gel he puts two shampoos outside my door.

I would rate it a 3 star hotel with clean and comfortable rooms.

reviews.title: Great location with very poor service
reviews.text:
We had booked with additional bed to be put in the room.  On check in they confirmed that this will be made available in 30 minutes. We reminded after three hours and got further assurance that this would be done immediately.  However after three more hours, around 10.30 pm, we were told extra beds were not available!!  On speaking to the duty manager, it took another 30 minutes to get the bed.

Same problem with housekeeping when we asked for an extra towel.

When one enters the hotel, there is no one at the doors most of the time to welcome the guests.

When checking in, I was give wrong information on parking fees (Was told it would be MYR 5 per day and they charged MYR 10 per day).  

Clearly their staff need substantial training in providing correct information as well as taking care of customers. Management is responsible for the poor situation there.
reviews.positives: Good location, close to the twin towers with good views.

reviews.title: ok hotel but great location
reviews.text: we were disapointed with the hotel itself, we went to the wrong wing and was pushed on quite rudley after we refused the upgrade to that wing extra so we went to other wing service on that side was a bit better but once again pushed for a room upgrade, the room itself was a good size but it was pretty run down our fridge didnt work, only 3 out of seven lights worked 3 were hanging out from the cieling! which is quite dangerous! on the good side the swimming pool was lovely but not warm, the breakfast had heaps of variety not alot of western food and they had silverside bacon, and a chocolate fountain which i thought was a bit over the top, it was a good location close to plenty of restaurants and the cable car and a short stroll to the train, close to the towers, probaly wouldnt stay there again i think i could get better value for my money elsewhere.

reviews.title: Decent Hotel but be cautious !!!!
reviews.text: Decent Hotel and good deal although they usually forget about the F&B credit they include in the offer. Secondly, the staff was totally not worthy of 5 star standards. I checked in my room and went out for some meetings and by the time I came back in the evening, they had given my room to another traveller and removed my luggage out of the room with the bell desk while my valuables (thankfully I had put them in the safebox) were still in the room. Upon confronting the front office on how they could commit such a blunder, they blamed it on their system but what I could not get is how they physically removed my luggage and never realised they were wrong or even apologetic about it. Ultimately the front office manager showed up after waiting a good 20 min and she apologised and finally put me in another room. I'm never staying at this hotel again.

reviews.title: Noisy executive suite
reviews.text:
Booked this room intentionally because I have a 1yo baby and 5yo kid, and this room is indeed very big and spacious. I make a remark to request a quiet room since we have kids and we also think we entitled a good rest for night time in a 5stars hotel and paying so much higher prices. However this room can hear all the noise from outside the road and worse is so so SO noisy during midnight from 2am to 4am. All of us couldn’t get a good rest that a hotel supposed to provide. Hotel receptionist never even inform us and we found out that is a problem always there from a bar tanner from the hotel. 
It is Ridiculous as I even requested A QUIET room, but they still arrange this room for me! The very basic that a hotel should provide but they never. We booked 2nights and both days we can’t even get any rest in the night. What a nightmare! 

Next is, this SUITE doesn’t even have any cutlery provided, and we packed some food and wanted to enjoy in our room, and requested cutlery from this 5STARs hotel and the staff mentioned to me it’s CHARGEABLE and it’s RM6 for a pair of disposable spoon and fork! Can you imagine a 5stars hotel don’t provide any cutlery in the room/suite! 

This is my second time stay in this hotel and I was quite happy for my 1st round and it is also the same room type last round but there is no such problem. 
However this round is very bad experience, a hotel doesn’t provide a comfortable quiet room which I can’t accept it!

reviews.title: Oldie but a goodie
reviews.text: Whilst the hotel is definitely old & in need of refurbishment, it was still clean, comfortable & a great option for an overnight stay close to KLCC. The gym is large with all imaginable (but old) machines for a solid workout, lovely large pool area & breakfast spread more than sufficient. We stayed in the west wing which was a bit of a walk to breakfast & gym, and unusually no mini bar (although menu option to order such items). It was a last minute booking upon changing our arrival into KL. Fikry on concierge was very knowledgeable and helpful in giving suggestions of places to go & to navigate around town. Definitely suggest using nearby public transport as traffic is terrible (all of the time it seems) which is easy & cheap. Would definitely consider staying again as it made for a good base to explore the city (versus being there to use the hotel for a "Staycation").

reviews.title: Old hotel, poor value for money
reviews.text:
I was disappointed with the quality of the room. The cupboard couldn't open properly. The electric plug for the desk (and my laptop) was under the desk so i had to crawl under to plug it in and i have bad knees so it wasn't convenient. Modern hotels now have plugs above the desk so it is easy to plug in. There was only one other plug i found to plug in my phone charger, while i expected at least one electric point by each bed. The room smelt musty. There was only ONE bath towel in the room so i would have had to call for another one but i didn't want to wait so we just shared. I felt the price I paid for the room as not worth it. Also there is a parking fee of RM10 a day (multiple entry) even if you are a hotel guest, which I thought was a bit much. 

Positive points? The bed was very comfortable. The pool is big (i saw from the window.. i didn't swim in it). Breakfast, which was included in the price, was adequate though breakfast is supposed to end at 11am and by 10.30am, a lot of the food was already finished and not replenished.

I probably would not come back to this hotel again.

reviews.title: Very Busy Hotel
reviews.text:
Stayed there for 3 nights during our holiday. The hotel is very popular located in between KLCC and Bukit Bintang with a convenient mono rail station right at the door. Mono Rail to Bukit Bintang is 2.5 ringit compared to a 20 ringit taxi so good saving there. The hotel is bustling with people especially air hostesses from Emirates. There are alot of people at the door and they seem to almost force you to take the hotel taxi (which is more expensive) then budget ones and look at you uncomfortably when you say no. Breakfast selection is superb but a little cramped for space. The pool area is nice and drinks are cheap priced for a good class hotel. Rooms are very modern at the lifestyle level and the twin tower views are spectacular.

The downside of our stay was the bell boy forgot to pack one of our suitcases onto the hotel taxi, so we had to pay an extra fare for another taxi to bring the suitcase back, I was astonished that the hotel was unwilling to cover this fee as they had made the mistake on the concierge desk initally and we had to nearly leave Malaysia without our baggage!

reviews.title: Best Fitness Center run by a Hotel
reviews.text: My deal via Agoda was without the breakfast and ultimately I have to spend additional US$18/person for the breakfast. Luckily the breakfast is good, lot of choices. Another downside for the hotel is the room is pretty run down and basic, carpet in the room (and corridor as well) is pretty old and dirty. Internet at fee and very expensive as well. No LCD TV but a small 21" CTV with limited 6 - 7 channels available (most are free channels and it is BORING)? If not because of the gym and swimming pool, I would rate it 2.5 stars instead of 4. But the location and the gym/pool pushes it up to 3, or max 3.5 stars. They should renovate the entire hotel room to draw more customers. Concorde Hotel right next to it is pretty good and value for money.
reviews.positives: 1) Best / Top Class Fitness Center for a full workout. Open 24 hours. Guest can entire the gym using the key card. 2) Very inviting swimming pool 3) Great breakfast
reviews.negatives: 1) breakfast is relatively expensive 2) room is pretty run down (can't believe they still use 21" ctv instead of lcd nowaday) 3) limited tv channel, if i remember correctly, only 6 - 7 channels available and most of it are boring, can't they spend a little more to upgrade the tv programme? is a 4 stars hotel... common.

reviews.title: Decent stay but not without complaint
reviews.text:
Except for one lady, every frontdesk staff I came into contact lacked service attitude.  Actually their other staff at restaurants and over the in-house phone services are okay.  Somehow the frontdesk people are an exception. When I checked out I had to argue over the charge on a phone call (MYR25 for a 4-minute call on a mobile number in KL area). The FD manager just told me it's hotel charge and insisted that I paid up. When she finally gave in with a waiver on that call, she just gave instruction to her staff to draft a refund voucher and turned away.  Didn't even throw me a look.  I took that moment and looked over the entire staff at the FD. None of them wore a smile at the guests. I then realised that it had been like that throughout the three days I spent there.

The run-down condition of the hotel has been mentioned by other reviewers.  While the pool looks great, the water actually feels and actually taste like a mixture of sweat and sun-tan lotion.  It feels greasy at the bottom.

To summarise this hotel doesn't deserve a 5-star rating.  I don't get the Marriott-level of  experience that one would expect from it.  It's just that the room rate is good but that doesn't make it standout from local/regional hotel brands.
reviews.positives: Good location - KLCC nearby and just two monorail stops from city centre. Big hotel with good facilities.
reviews.negatives: Bad, bad front-desk staff service

reviews.title: Mediocre service & need renovation
reviews.text:
I booked a tower-view room at this hotel on the new year's eve and the new year day. When I arrived at the hotel at around 6:00 pm on 31 Dec, the staff at the front desk told me that the room was not ready and it would take another 2 hours for their staff to complete cleaning of the room before I could check in. Did they expect guests to wander in the street until 8 pm before they could come and check-in? The staff at the front desk simply could not offer any help and kept saying that cleaning was not his responsibilities. After a heated debate at the front desk, another staff came take over my case and offered an option to upgrade my room to a pool-view suite. After I checked in, I wrote a letter to the hotel management expressing my dissatisfaction. On the 2nd day of my stay, a plate of dessert pastry was placed in the room. I believed that represented the hotel's response to my comment.

I originally chose this hotel for its tower-view room and its service. At the end, I got neither of them.

The hotel needs a bit of maintenance work to be done. I experienced a malfunctioned key card reader in the elevator and this made an elevator-full of guests like idiots. The TV reception inside the room was also not good.

reviews.title: 5-star hotel with 3-star service
reviews.text:
Location is ideal for Bukit Bintang and KLCC, and the pool area is nice an relaxing.

However, disappointed in the service for a 5-star hotel in KL, and first bad experience of Renaissance in Asia.  

The toilet didn't flush, even after having someone come to fix it, and the breakfast left a lot to be desired, not a great choice, nor enough seats.  

If you are shown to an empty table, don't leave it, as someone else will also be shown to it whilst you are getting your food - even if you leave your stuff on the table!  This happened twice one day!

On the plus side breakfast is served till 11, so you have enough time to find another table!

reviews.text: Be sure to specify smoking or non-smoking room when you make the reservation. As non-smokers, we were told that no non-smoking rooms were available on arrival. The first room allocated smelled as if the world cigar smoking championships had just been held there. On complaining, we were offered a second room that was marginally better, but also a smoker. In both rooms, the furniture was old and in one case broken, so it is to be hoped some refurbishment is in the pipeline. The promised newspaper in English also failed to appear until asked for. Disappointing for a 5-star hotel, but since the cost was modest, Ok value for money overall.

reviews.title: Petronas Visit
reviews.text: As a first time to visit Malaysia we wanted to see the Petronas and KL tower. Renaissance is a perfect location from both towers. To Petronas you can go walking 10 minutes and even have a view of the twin towers from your room in the West Wing. Then the KL tower is also just around 20 minutes walk from the hotel. The hotel is good in terms of lcoation becuase it is not far from the subway which we always used going around markets and sigt seeing. Also there is the rail station which is near to the hotel accessible to all sight seeing destinations. It is advisable to used these transporations than the taxi because in the city the taxis are charging too much and not using the meter fare.  In Kuala Lumpur taxi are being contracted depending your desinations. So it is better to have a map and take a ride from the sub way and rail station which are safe and clean. The city is clean and pleasant. Food are good and everyboy speaks english so anytime you are lost there is always somebody to ask for direction and assistance. Overall our Trip to Malaysia is pleasant and a thing to add to our album is a nice and clean country.
reviews.positives: The view of the room is great. Directly facing the petronas and the KL tower. Accessible to the rail station, sub-way and the twin towers and other bars at night. Very near to the tourism office.
reviews.negatives: The rooms in the west wing are ok but not so accesorize not too much things to be used as in the 5-star hotels. The east wing the windows are not double sealed; you can hear the noise of the cars at night even you are in the high floors.

reviews.title: Eid in malaysia
reviews.text: Renaissance Hotel in terms of location is the best to stay in. First there is teh Tourism Office which is 5mins. away from the hotel. You can use the internet complimentary for 15 minutes. There are airlines also which you can book to go the islands of Malaysia like LAngkawi and Penang. They are friendly and assisting, they have a beutiful garden as well were you can take pictures. Then 10mins walk you will reach the beautiful Petronas Towers, nice shopping and food. At night a great view of the twin towers is breath taking. Also if you are in the mood for walking the KL towers can be aproximize by walking around 25-30 mins from the hotel. For roaming around the city it is advised to used the subways and rail stations. Going to the China Market and Central Market, by subway it will be just two stations away from teh Dang Wangi station which is evry near to the hotel. At night if you want to have some fun the street going to the Hrad Rock is awake at night, and if you walk more all the pubs abd bar at night are located in that area. Barbacue were tasty and cheap... drinks are over flowing and good bands all over the place. Spending Eid in Malaysia is a time to remember and to add to our memories because you can see them still wearing there traditional dress during the holidays celebrating there holidays on a very special occassion for the muslims.
reviews.positives: Waking distance to the Twin Towers and 15 mins to KL Tower. Near to the sub-wy and rail stations. If you need assistance to go all around Malaysia, tehre tourism office is just 5 mins away from the hotel.
reviews.negatives: Hotel accesories are not as expected from a 5-star hotel. They only put shampoo and lotion. No toothbrush, conditioner, vanity box and etc. Poor presentation of the room.

reviews.title: Family Friendly Hotel
reviews.text: We stayed at this family-friendly hotel and appreciated the many facilities available—it’s great for all ages. However, we experienced a major issue during our stay: loud motorcycle noise from 2am to 5am, which resulted in a sleepless and very unpleasant night. Additionally, I had requested a birthday deco for my husband’s celebration, but unfortunately, the hotel did not prepare it. This was disappointing for what was meant to be a special occasion.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
It was a stressful short getaway with a lot of hiccups which we do not expect and foresee with so many good reviews on the internet.

1. There were different pricings for the same room and package stated on Agoda website itself (but unfortunately the cheaper option were "hidden", as it can only be seen if you click "see more". When confronted, nothing much can be done as the earlier booking was stated as non refundable.
2. Upon arrival, bookings were made for King bed on the Agoda app, but it was shown as Twin Bed on the hotel end. It was rectify on the spot albeit it took awhile.
3. That's when our nightmare begins - Water heater was not working. We waited for hours for the technician to sort things out and even had to follow them to different vacant rooms to test the water temperature ourselves and yet all had the same issue! We had no choice but to stay put since it was already way past our kids bedtime.
4. In addition to the hot water issue, the water pressure was also low, making it difficult to have a proper shower, let alone ensure my kids were comfortable.
5. One of the bathtub even had cockroach peeking out from the tub when we were checking other available rooms - needless to say we didn't even bother checking if the room temperature was acceptable to us.
6. There were promises that an email will be sent in terms of late check out and points as a courtesy for what has happened. It has been days yet we did not receive ANYTHING AT ALL.

The combination of these issues had left us extremely dissatisfied with our stay, and I believe it reflects poorly on the high standards we associate with the Renaissance and Marriot brand.
reviews.room_type: Deluxe Twin Room

reviews.title: Disappointing Experience - Inconsistent Service and Unacceptable Cleanliness
reviews.text:
We recently stayed at this hotel for 4 days with two adults and two children, and unfortunately, our experience was far from satisfactory.

Inconsistent Cleaning Routine: Despite informing the hotel that we had four occupants, we were consistently given only two towels after every room cleaning. This lack of attention to detail was frustrating and inconvenient throughout our stay.

Maintenance Issues: On the first day, we reported a leak from the bathroom that was dripping into the toilet bowl which was hazard for kids to enter through the bathroom. Despite following up during our stay, the issue was never addressed, and the leak continued throughout our visit.

Unacceptable Cleanliness: The pillowcases and quilt covers in our room were stained with what appeared to be blood and dirt. This is absolutely unacceptable for a hotel of any standard, and certainly not something we expected to encounter during our stay.

Overall, we were very disappointed with the level of service and cleanliness, and we will not be returning to this hotel in the future. We hope the management takes these issues seriously and improves the quality of their offerings.
reviews.room_type: Deluxe, Guest Room, 2 Twin, Sofa Bed
