reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: Location not great with nothing to eat within walking distance. Lack of ramp to hotel restaurant, not very baby stroller friendly (but they will help you carry). Parking is not free, rm5 per night if not mistaken. Rm50 penalty for lost parking card. Quite unreasonable imo considering most hotels of this calibre offer free parking. Room price is quite high for what they dont offer. Breakfast was decent

reviews.title: Cockroach in the toilet
reviews.text: I saw small cockroaches in the toilet every morning and it's really disgusting. Reported to the staff and they said they will do something but I still see it the next morning. Not sure if they are in the room but cannot expect from a 5 star hotel.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: Nice room and fantastic klcc view but no complimentary parking.

reviews.title: Stuck with unrenovated room in old tower
reviews.text: A bit of a lottery in getting newly renovated or unrenovated  room and naturally I got into an unrenovated room. Not happy with the old bed, room furniture have seen better days, poor buffet breakfast selection and ambiance felt more like being at a crowded food court. Located at the intersection of 2 major roads so try to get rooms facing the pool at the back to avoid traffic noise. No cafes, street food, shops, or restaurants nearby all requiring a walk of at least several hundred metres. The best part of the hotel is that it has a large resort style pool with plenty of lounge chairs and a swim-up bar but you'll have to take a hit with the 5-star prices for drinks at this place.

reviews.title: East section
reviews.text: Check in staff ask for 100rm extra to update to West section ? ???? Otherwise they will  give me a room in east section !!!!!!! Don’t stay in east section it’s very old & dirty

reviews.title: Reservation Bad
reviews.text: I booked West wing non smoking room with breakfast but I got East wing smoking room at first then I complained. They came to my spray the room but it still had bad smell. Finally they changed the room for me to non-smoking but still in East wing. Furthermore, when I checked out they were gonna charge me for breakfast. I am very disappointed with the service and tax charge because they said, it is changing very day. Oh I have never seen any country changing tax charge everyday. Do I look stupid! hello. I booked the room with Agoda and the price is included.

reviews.title: Great property but bad service
reviews.text:
Location was good. The hotel ambience is good. The room was big, clean with comfy beds and an excellent AC. Only wish the service and food could have been better. 

Food: the buffet breakfast seemed limited and lacking in taste. We also tried their room service a few times and it was underwhelming.

Service: any request took almost an hour to be tendered. The housekeeping was shabby.

Will prefer to give other properties a chance next time I am here.

reviews.title: Never try it again
reviews.text: However they renewed the hotel recently, there is connecting door between rooms which is not able to control the sound. If your neighbour talk in midnight, you do not have any chance except sitting till they finish their conversation. you can sleep after that.

reviews.text: I am very sure I left  a pack of vitamins and a power bank in the room but when I called housekeeping's lost and found they said none was ever found.

reviews.title: Grand Lady Of Yesteryear
reviews.text: Used to be one of the popular meeting place of business travelers to Kuala Lumpur, the grand lady shows her age not only physically but spiritually in management and service performance. The couldn't-care-less doorman repeatedly educates me that the blue taxis by the hotel entrance are their "hotel taxis" and the only ones available for guests...

reviews.title: The Chinese Lady at the business center was rude!
reviews.text: The Chinese Lady at the business center was so rude. I think her name is loong coz her email add that she had given me to send my airticket was prety_loong. She doesdeposit at the hotel. That time i didnt bring my wallet and i told her ill pay the hotel upon check out coz i had deposit at the hotel and she told me i dont have it. This ugly bitch ruined my morning that time... They dont have fixed rate for the taxi everyday they were giving me different price for the taxi.
reviews.positives: Huge swimming pool, clean room, nice breakfast!!!

reviews.text: Good

reviews.title: Outdated opulence
reviews.text: I would have rated this a 5 star hotel had I visited in 1995, but this is 2015. The Club Rooms and Facilities are the epitome of 90's kitsch, complete with gilded wooden furniture, bevel edged mirrors and over-elaborate upholstery. A very tired looking hotel in desperate need of renovation. Service was mediocre, I had to change rooms twice as they failed to note my stated preferences submitted with the booking. Food was unimpressive, but the wifi was convenient with good coverage.

reviews.title: short bur pleasant stay
reviews.text: I stayed only for one night to attend an event which was held in the same hotel. Therefore this hotel provided the most appropriate location. The hotel is old but well maintained. It is located within walking distance to many attractive locations such as Menara Tower, KLCC or Hard Rock.

reviews.title: Weekend stay
reviews.text: Breakfast was disappointed. Fruits was not very fresh, selection was limited.

reviews.title: Satisfactory but with room for improvement
reviews.text:
Had a satisfactory stay. Found the room where I stayed in (West Wing) could use some sprucing up. Furnishing and fittings were worn and tired looking. Faucet was leaky and bathtub was a bit dirty.
During my stay, only one (1) of the breakfast restaurant was available so the place was packed during the morning rush.
reviews.positives: Friendly staff, nearness to Monorail and central location.

reviews.title: Not that great after all
reviews.text: My stay in Renaissance Kuala Lumpur Hotel was OK. The room looks very old yet the staff told me the room is just renovated. The breakfast was just OK only. Lack of varieties I think. So overall the price that I paid is not really that worth it with the condition of the old room

reviews.title: Great stay with kids

However, it looks like it needed some renovation to keep up to date with current trend. Buffet offer variety and indeed tasty, though, price was somewhat on the highside.

The toilet leaked from the flush button. Defenantly not up to 5 star standard. Other than that they have an amazing gym and a great pool. The buffet breakfast is great too.

reviews.title: THE EXPERIENCE AT THE HOTEL
reviews.text: GOOD VIEW OF THE PATRONAS TWIN TOWERS FROM MY ROOM. There are many malls near the hotel if you like to shop, and it is easy to catch a taxi to go to the malls. Many shopping malls are within 10 minutes by taxi. There are many varieties of good and delicious local foods at the malls and the prices are reasonable.

reviews.negatives: I HAD TO CALL THE FRONT DESK TO CHANGE A NEW ROOM BECAUSE I FOUND A COCKROACH ( ABOUT 1" LONG ) ON THE BED ON THE 1ST NIGHT WHEN I WAS ABOUT TO GO TO BED. I HAD TO MAKE A 2ND CALL BEFORE I WAS GIVEN  THE KEY TO TRANSFER TO ANOTHER ROOM WITH A CLEAN BED.

reviews.negatives: place falling apart, toilet roll holder half damage, shower tap easy to drop, nothing works well in bathroom, nothing specail about the room, lobby was well done, had to pay for internet

reviews.title: good location
reviews.text:
Excellent location just minutes away from the twin tower and 50 meters from a sky train station. Great public area and rooms. Comfort is there but some aspect look tired and need to be updated.
Wonderful huge swimming pool but completely unattended, staff will look at you but will never assist you in any way nor take your drink-food order, you must go to the bar to get something !!! 
Poor image of service and not too good for their revenues....
Concierge are greats people but be careful....I have seen a nice advertising for an half day tour in some "villages" with a lot of wonderful picture but at the end we spent our time in some unwanted shopping with no interest at all in the industrial suburb of the Kuala Lumpur !!!
We ended in a most dirty temple I have ever seen located near a high way.
Their in-room internet connection is at the highest price ever practice for nothing special.
Shame in a country which often advertised it self as a I.T place to be.
Room service is so long that I lost my appetite when the food reached me completely cold !!!
Any way i will still go to this hotel because of it s location but I will definitely avoid all those poor expensive services.
reviews.negatives: staff attitude

reviews.title: A view from the top  floor
reviews.text:
This hotel is really to big for holiday. Located close to the monorail station, tourist information Malaysia and 15 minutes away from Petronas Tower shopping malls. The sorroundings is filled with half finished building ruines, a memory of a busted property bubble. During our stay angry radical moslems rioters torched churches, we could see the smoke from our window in the morning. 

The hotel is  expensive considering that you have to make an upgrade to club level to get the nice calm ambience for relaxing and reading. Probably we will stay at Mandarin Oriental, wich have much better food and WSJ as complementary, next time in Kuala Lumpur.
reviews.positives: Nice friendly staff, efficient , making best of what is a typical Marriot style big impersonal conference hotel.
reviews.negatives: The normal breakfastbufffet was big but not very well orginized. We paid exrtra to have an upgrade to to club class wich had thier own smaller but higher quality buffet o the 25th floor. No ATM in hotel, we had to walk to Petronas Mall to get cash from our gold card. No foreign newspapers as FT or WSJ at hotel.

reviews.title: Great value
reviews.text: It was a nice stay for us, we really enjoyed the location, the view from our room is amazing, definitely value for money. The service was excellent as well. Definitely one of the best value hotels to stay in Kuala Lumpur. However, improvement should be made on the quality of the room and the amenities.

reviews.title: Kuala Lumpa
reviews.text: You go through seurity checks like airport everytime you access the hotel, which can be annoying. OK location. You need to cross a very busy road to get to MRT. There are many entrances to the subway and its not clear which line it is. You may end up walking up and down a few steep stairways! In the end we used taxis. Less hassle and you can almost get anywhere for 10-15 riggets (£1.50-£2.00). If you do use taxis make sure you get the driver to drop you at the correct wing of the hotel or you could end up walking for about 10 mins to get to the other end.

Hotel very slow when you checking in.
Hotel tried to charge us again for our room on our tab when we checked out. So make sure you check your bill.

reviews.title: Bad service
reviews.text:
I requested a baby cot in advance for my 4-month-old baby when making the reservation. Upon check-in, the staff initially assured me it would be arranged, but later another staff informed us that all baby cots were fully occupied. We wasted quite a lot of time going back and forth with staff and finally had to meet the manager before one was provided.

This experience was stressful and inconvenient. For safety reasons, I believe baby cots should not be on a “first come, first serve” basis. If the hotel is unable to guarantee one, guests should be informed at the time of booking so parents can make alternative arrangements, such as bringing their own.

I hope the hotel can improve this process, as it will make family stays more comfortable and reassuring.
reviews.room_type: Deluxe, Guest Room, 2 Twin, Sofa Bed

reviews.title: Staff service needs improvement
reviews.text:
This is our first time staying at the Renaissance KL. Our stay was mostly uneventful, but we were very disappointed at the level of service and professionalism of the staff here which was well below expectations for a 5-star hotel.

Most of the staff don't even bother to greet hello. Some of them congregate and chit-chat during work hours and blocks the customers. There was a lady at West Wing Lobby Lounge who frowned angrily at us when we asked for the menu.

We did, thankfully, come across a handful of excellent staff who brightened our stay - Sohar, Dulhan and Koay from the Club Lounge, and Budi Ashraf from the West Wing Lobby Lounge.

In conclusion, I wished that we had encountered better service from the staff. I really wanted to like the place after reading great reviews. Unfortunately, the don't-care attitude of the staff really dampened our Christmas mood.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: I have stayed in this hotel thrice. One in Feb this year. To my utter surprise, the hotel standards went downhill.  I was really upset. Was given the East wing because West wing was said to be fully booked. East wing is older building but didn't thought it'd be that bad till i open the door to the room. I was so shocked. Everything is old. Bathtub ironing board and even the iron itself has worn off. I was very disappointed because this time i brought my sister and hail praise to Renaissance because of my past 2 stays here. I was very upset. Bathroom is super slippery. Bfast food standard has dropped. I am speechless.  Thankfully, despite all the horrible room amenities, the staffs are superb. They are warm and friendly. Some recognized us, and we felt like home, except for their rooms. 2 nights was a dreadful stay. Sorry.. next.
